title: Sonnet 60 by William Shakespeare | line by line explanation in Hindi | Summary
description:
Few poems dare to look time directly in the face and argue back — but in Sonnet 60, William Shakespeare does exactly that, counting out human life in minutes the way a clock counts seconds, relentless and indifferent. Written as part of his legendary sonnet sequence, this poem wrestles with the themes of time's destruction and the immortality of poetry, making it essential reading for BA English Honours students under DU, SOL, and IGNOU syllabi. Shakespeare's audacious claim — that his verse will outlive death itself — hits differently when you realise he wrote this over four hundred years ago, and here you still are, reading it.
